output:
Looking at this as a straightforward probability problem:

**Given:**
- P(College+) = 45%, so P(HighSchool‚àí) = 55%
- P(HighSchool‚àí and BlackHair) = 17%
- P(College+ and BlackHair) = 18%

**Conditional probabilities:**
- P(BlackHair | HighSchool‚àí) = 17% / 55% ‚âà **30.9%**
- P(BlackHair | College+) = 18% / 45% = **40.0%**

**Yes** ‚Äî the chance of black hair is larger when observing college degree or higher (40.0% vs 30.9%).
